front: Short Story: Achille Papin introduces the title character via letter; he had kissed one of the dean's daughters during a duet from Don Giovanni
Short Story: the title character declares 'a great artist, Madame, is never poor' and is told 'how you will enchant the angels'
Short Story: a turtle arriving in the kitchen frightens the devout Protestant sisters; General Loewenhielm exclaims he is eating 'Blinis Demidoff'
Short Story: the dean's daughters are named after Martin Luther and Philipp Melanchthon
Short Story: a cardinal is revealed to be the valet Kasparson, who tells 'The Wine of the Tetrarch'; Miss Nat-og-Dag compares herself to Scheherazade
Short Story: Timon of Assens learns his singing is popular only because he's illegitimate; told salt water (sweat, tears, sea) is 'the cure for everything'
Short Story: Miss Nat-og-Dag almost cut off her breasts but was stopped by seeing a painting of nymphs in a mirror
Memoir: Denys Finch Hatton died in a plane crash; his brother placed an obelisk with lines from 'The Rime of the Ancient Mariner'
Memoir: Kamante, a sick boy with leg sores, becomes the narrator's cook; Berkeley Cole drinks champagne every morning
Memoir: Ngugi wa Thiong'o called this work 'one of the most dangerous books ever written' for its condescending depiction of Kenya
Short Story: the De Coninck sisters Fanny and Eliza converse with the ghost of their brother Morton, who tells of his pirate career
work: Other Stories from Seven Gothic Tales

front: Short Story: the Prioress of Cloister Seven schemes to marry her homosexual nephew Boris to the unwilling Athena; the Prioress switches places with the title animal
work: Other Stories from Seven Gothic Tales

front: Short Story: the opera singer Pellegrina Leoni appears as multiple identities (milliner, Madame Rosalba, Olalla) chased through an Alpine snowstorm
work: Other Stories from Seven Gothic Tales

front: Short Story: the impotent Prince Potenziani asks Count Nino to impregnate his wife; the cross-dressing Agnese interrupts a duel
work: Other Stories from Seven Gothic Tales

front: Short Story: a peasant woman dies saving her son Goske from prison by mowing a field in one day
work: Other Stories from Seven Gothic Tales

front: Short Story: Baron von Brackel recalls sleeping with an unknown prostitute; fifteen years later recognizes her skull at an artist's studio
work: Other Stories from Seven Gothic Tales

front: Author: classified women as housewives, guardian angels, dancers, or witches in the essay 'Daguerreotypes'
